app.name: BuildingLens
app.tagline: Inspection reports and public data, turned into a building risk view.

common.mockNotice: No API key detected: answers run in mock mode. Add a key to .env for real answers.

provider.label: AI provider
provider.mockTooltip: Running in mock mode: answers are generated locally without an API key.

landing.title: Turn inspection reports into a clear building risk view
landing.subtitle: See which buildings carry the most risk, open any one as a full dossier, and ask the whole portfolio a question, from the inspection reports you already hold.
landing.problemBody: Asset managers and insurers sit on stacks of long, heterogeneous inspection reports. Each one is written differently, the important defects are buried in dense prose, and there is no quick way to compare risk across a portfolio. Reading them all by hand does not scale.
landing.featureExtractBody: Pull every defect out of a PDF report, with its discipline, location and severity.
landing.featureScoreBody: Roll the defects up into a single risk score per building, weighted by severity.
landing.featureAskBody: Question the portfolio in everyday words and get answers grounded in the reports, with citations.
landing.howItWorks: BuildingLens runs on public data: EUBUCCO Luxembourg buildings, STATEC construction permits, and synthetic inspection reports. Everything is reproducible from the

notFound.title: Page not found
notFound.body: The page you are looking for does not exist or has moved.

footer.tagline: BuildingLens turns public construction data into a building risk view.

search.title: Ask the portfolio
search.subtitle: Ask a question in plain language. The answer is grounded in the inspection reports and cites its sources.
search.placeholder: Ask a question about the buildings...
search.searchingNote: Retrieving the relevant report passages and grounding the answer.
search.groundedNote: Answers are grounded in the inspection reports and cite the passages they used.
search.noAnswer: No answer yet. Type a question above or pick an example.
search.streamFallback: The stream was interrupted; showing the partial answer.

scope.empty: No buildings match this scope. Clear a filter to widen it.

exampleList: Which buildings have critical fire-safety defects?
What are the structural defects in the highest-risk building?
Summarise the roofing problems across the portfolio

portfolio.title: Portfolio
portfolio.subtitle: All buildings ranked by risk score, with defect counts.
portfolio.name: Name

tips.buildings: Total buildings in the portfolio, ingested from EUBUCCO Luxembourg open data.
tips.totalDefects: All defects extracted from the inspection reports across every building.
tips.criticalDefects: Defects rated critical: an immediate safety or structural concern.
tips.riskChart: Risk score per building, highest first. The score combines how many defects a building has with how severe they are.

ingest.title: Import a report
ingest.subtitle: Upload an inspection PDF. BuildingLens extracts the defects, recomputes the risk score, and indexes the text for search.
ingest.registryHint: Search a fixed, reproducible sample of 200 EUBUCCO registry buildings by commune. Footprint, height, coordinates and commune are real; the name and street address are synthetic placeholders. Pick a building, then attach its inspection PDF.
ingest.registryEmpty: No registry buildings available right now.
ingest.searchPlaceholderHint: Type at least 2 letters of a commune.
ingest.noMatch: No building in this sample matches that commune.
ingest.mockNote: No defects were extracted: the app is running in mock mode without an API key. Configure a key in
ingest.emptyBody: Import your first inspection report to extract its defects, score the building and make it searchable.

building.noCoordinates: No public coordinates available for this building.

tips.riskScore: Risk score from 0 to 100. It weighs critical defects most, then major, then minor, on a saturating curve so a building can approach but never exceed 100.
tips.critical: Critical: an immediate risk to safety or structural integrity. Address without delay.
tips.major: Major: a significant defect that needs planned remediation.
tips.minor: Minor: a cosmetic or low-impact issue to monitor.
tips.byDiscipline: How the defects split across building disciplines such as structure, roofing or building services.
tips.bySeverity: The share of defects at each severity rating.
tips.defectList: Every defect extracted from the inspection report, each with the sentence it came from. Click a column to sort, or filter by severity.
tips.downloads: Generate a client-ready report with the executive summary and the full defect table. Excel keeps the table interactive; PDF is print-ready.
tips.askAbout: Ask in plain language. The answer is grounded only in this building's report and cites the passages it used.
tips.map: Approximate position of the building in Luxembourg, from its public coordinates.
tips.use: Building use, from OpenStreetMap or EUBUCCO's model depending on the building. Not a cadastral declaration.
tips.floors: Number of floors, model-estimated by EUBUCCO and shown rounded.
tips.footprint: Ground footprint area, computed from the real building outline (EUBUCCO, EPSG:3035).
tips.height: Building height. From the Luxembourg cadastre for most buildings, model-estimated for the rest (EUBUCCO).

settings.title: Settings
settings.subtitle: Choose the AI provider used for answers, set its key and model, and check the connection.
settings.apiKeyHint: Stored on the server and never shown again. Leave blank to keep the current key.
settings.mockWarning: An online provider is selected but no valid key was found, so answers are running in mock mode. Add a key and save to use the real provider.
settings.authBody: Single sign-on with Azure Entra ID is planned, so the portfolio can be shared with named users behind your organisation's identity. This panel is documentation only for now.
